response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise and large-scale systems experience, they critically lack the required 8 years of C++ programming (resume shows only ~6 months professional work at National Instruments plus thesis work). Additionally, their career has focused on full-stack web and application development rather than cloud compute infrastructure, distributed systems, and hardware architecture that are central to this technical leadership role.
strengths: 16+ years of professional software development with proven ability to design and ship large-scale systems handling millions of transactions
Demonstrated technical leadership through staff-level roles, workshop creation, mentoring initiatives, and successful open-source projects
Full-stack technical versatility across multiple languages and technologies, with some infrastructure exposure (Kubernetes, AWS CDK, microservices)
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (approximately 6 months professional work) falls far short of the mandatory 8-year requirement
Career trajectory centered on web, application, and full-stack development rather than cloud compute platforms and infrastructure
No demonstrated experience with compute technologies, hardware architecture, or production-level cloud platform engineering
